summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #616 where the exec tool ignores `default_timeout_secs` and `max_output_bytes` config values, always using hardcoded defaults of 30s and 200KB.
summary.outcome: Implemented two builder methods on ExecTool, wired config values in server.rs, added 4 tests (41 total passing), resolved a PR comment about hardcoded schema description, and merged a clean PR (#617).

repo: ExecTool follows a `with_*` builder pattern — 7 such methods existed before this fix.
`max_output_bytes` was already wired for remote/node execution (~line 3547 in server.rs) but not for local execution — this fix brings local execution to parity.
Config fields for exec (`default_timeout_secs`, `max_output_bytes`) were already parsed and validated in schema.rs/validate.rs; only the wiring to the tool instance was missing.
The repo has a known pre-existing macOS build failure in `llama-cpp-sys-2` (tracked as `moltis-csq`) that causes `just lint` to fail; clippy on individual crates is the workaround.
server.rs uses fully-qualified `std::time::Duration` rather than a `use` import.

code.finding: Added `with_default_timeout(Duration)` and `with_max_output_bytes(usize)` builder methods following existing `with_*` pattern.
ExecTool is constructed here; added `.with_default_timeout` and `.with_max_output_bytes` chained from `config.tools.exec`.
`parameters_schema` hardcoded 'default 30' in the timeout description string — fixed to remove the hardcoded value since the actual default now comes from config.

workflow: Use `cargo clippy -p <crate>` to avoid the pre-existing llama-cpp macOS build failure when linting.
Greptile bot left a useful PR comment catching the stale hardcoded description in `parameters_schema` — worth watching for automated review feedback.
Beads issue was created and closed as part of the workflow before committing.
Branch was an ephemeral worktree branch (`veiled-hour`) intended for local merge to main.

friction: server.rs uses fully-qualified `std::time::Duration` instead of a `use` import, requiring a fixup edit after the initial implementation.
Clippy via `just lint` fails on macOS due to pre-existing `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake build error, forcing per-crate clippy invocations as a workaround.
Background clippy task completed after the main work was already done, requiring a TaskOutput read to confirm it was just the known pre-existing issue.
open_items: The pre-existing `llama-cpp-sys-2` CMake build failure on macOS (tracked as `moltis-csq`) remains unresolved and blocks `just lint`.
The `parameters_schema` timeout description now omits the default value entirely — could be improved to dynamically reflect the configured default rather than just removing the hardcoded string.
